task: Create a no-write Editor planning brief for one approved Scout opportunity.
prompt: Act as the LLM Editor planner for lastzguides.com. Return a planning brief only. Do not write final public page copy, HTML, or patch specs. Preserve the existing page template, cluster role, canonical claims, internal routing, SEO/LLM eligibility, and owner approval gate. Treat analytics as signals, not proof. Normally return exact_replacements as an empty array. Only include exact_replacements when the deterministic context contains enough current source text to copy exact_old literally and the replacement is narrow enough for later owner review. exact_replacements are draft proposal data only, not approval, not a Patch Spec, and not permission to edit files. Use plain ASCII English only. Return JSON only.

selected_opportunity.rationale: Owner approved no-write LLM worker chain for research opportunity only.
selected_opportunity.player_value: Players get a more useful research path and faster understanding of what to prioritize first.
selected_opportunity.duplication_risk: Medium; must stay distinct from costs, hero training, and peace shield guidance.
selected_opportunity.next_step: Run the no-write Editor and Reviewer stages from this approved decision snapshot.

deterministic_proposal.title: GSC opportunity review: Last Z Research Guide — Best Research Order, Peace Shield, and T10 Path
deterministic_proposal.source_reference: GSC weekly 2026-05-03: page opportunity and query-page signals
deterministic_proposal.notes: research.html shows meaningful impressions but middling CTR, plus a rising research priority query. This suggests the page likely needs clearer positioning of order, scope, and progression value rather than new content.
deterministic_proposal.player_value: Players get a more useful research path and faster understanding of what to prioritize first.
deterministic_proposal.duplication_risk: Medium; must stay distinct from costs, hero training, and peace shield guidance.
deterministic_proposal.next_step: Route to human review to test whether the page can better answer research priority intent within current structure.
evidence: GSC page signal: research.html had 12710 impressions, 597 clicks, 4.70% CTR, avg position 7.27.
Rising query: `last z research priority` gained 11 impressions in the last 7-day comparison window.

site_fit.primary_user_job: Improve query-to-page match and first-screen usefulness for `last z research guide` searchers.
site_fit.archetype_reason: Existing page is `cornerstone-guide` in the `Research` cluster; Scout should prefer updating the existing page before proposing new content.

constraints: Use existing page template and navigation patterns.
Treat GSC data as a signal, not proof that a rewrite is needed.
reject_if: The query intent is already better served by another canonical page.
The proposed change would blur cluster role separation.
The improvement cannot be expressed without changing a cornerstone page beyond the approved scope.

guardrails: No content files may be edited by this worker.
Do not generate final user-visible page copy.
Do not create a patch plan or Patch Spec.
exact_replacements, when present, are proposal-only candidates that still require propose + owner approval + apply-approved.
Every exact_replacements item must set owner_approval_required to true.
Do not mutate backlog, manifests, PRs, or production state.
Owner approval is required before any user-visible content change.
Use plain ASCII English only in every string field.

brief_summary.description: Short no-write summary of the recommended content planning direction.

first_screen_plan.description: Planning guidance for the first screen. Do not write final public copy.

exact_replacements.description: Optional draft exact replacement candidates. These are proposal-only and still require owner approval.
